item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll inspect the three source modules and make the smallest schema/model/query changes, then run the test suite against the configured database.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs && git status --short"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs && git status --short"
/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "accounts|preferredPayment|paymentMethod|accountTier" src package.json && rg --files src'
/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "accounts|preferredPayment|paymentMethod|accountTier" src package.json && rg --files src'

item.text: The enterprise tables store entity fields in JSONB documents, so I’m adding an optional database check for the JSON key plus model validation for the four allowed values. Existing documents without the key remain valid.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'npm test'
/bin/zsh -lc 'npm test'

item.text: The test exposed an important schema detail: `accounts` is not a JSONB `doc` table in this enterprise generator, despite the starter model/query assuming that shape. I’ll inspect the live table metadata and align the implementation to the actual schema.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "node --input-type=module -e \"import { withDb } from './src/db.mjs'; await withDb(async c => { const r = await c.query(\\\"SELECT column_name, data_type, udt_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema = current_schema() AND table_name = 'accounts' ORDER BY ordinal_position\\\"); console.log(r.rows); });\""

/bin/zsh -lc "node --input-type=module -e \"import { withDb } from './src/db.mjs'; await withDb(async c => { const r = await c.query(\\\"SELECT column_name, data_type, udt_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema = current_schema() AND table_name = 'accounts' ORDER BY ordinal_position\\\"); console.log(r.rows); });\""

item.text: The implementation is now aligned with the benchmark’s JSON-document API while preserving the enterprise tables’ required relational columns. A previous failed test left its fixture account in the isolated benchmark schema; I’ll clear that test residue and rerun.
